chunk_summary: Stunting, a marker of chronic undernutrition, is defined as height-for-age below -2 SD from WHO standards. India faces a high burden of stunting, wasting, and anemia in children under 5 years, as reported by NFHS-5 and CNNS benchmarks.
learning_objectives: Understand the epidemiologic definition of stunting and its classification criteria.
Analyze the national burden of stunting and related conditions in India using NFHS-5 and CNNS data.
Learn the importance of accurate measurement techniques and corrected age for preterm infants.
Identify determinants and red flags for growth faltering in pediatric practice.
key_facts: Stunting is defined as height-for-age below -2 SD from WHO child growth standards; severe stunting is < -3 SD.
NFHS-5 (2019–21) reports high prevalence of stunting, wasting, and anemia among children under 5 years in India.
CNNS (2016–18) provides biomarker data on micronutrient deficits and inflammation pathways.
Corrected age is essential for preterm infants to avoid false labeling of stunting during infancy.
WHO growth reference charts for ages 5–19 years or IAP charts are recommended for school-age children.
reference_quote: NFHS-5 (2019–21) reports a high national burden among children under 5 years, with stunting and wasting remaining common and anemia extremely prevalent in 6–59 month children.
document_key_topics: National burden of stunting and wasting in India
Measurement techniques and corrected age for preterm infants
Role of biomarkers in assessing micronutrient deficits
